The release is the first time an American AI company has launched a frontier model under a government-managed access list, a step beyond the voluntary pre-release review framework Trump’s AI executive order established […] This story continues at The Next Web]]> TikTok is building a super app, and most of its users have not noticed yet https://thenextweb.com/news/tiktok-super-app-shop-travel-fintech-world-cup Fri, 26 Jun 2026 17:43:39 +0000 TheNextWeb=e614850c9f96ea1c08e098e7e156fa0d TikTok is no longer just a video app. The platform has spent the past year adding hotel bookings, in-app commerce, sports hubs, casual games, microdramas, and a fintech licence application, building out the infrastructure of what the industry calls a “super app,” a single destination that handles tasks users currently spread across a dozen different […] This story continues at The Next Web]]> A single config file in a cloned repository could steal your AWS credentials through Amazon Q Developer https://thenextweb.com/news/amazon-q-developer-mcp-flaw-aws-credentials-stolen Fri, 26 Jun 2026 16:49:29 +0000 TheNextWeb=d35fca311ea005344992006626f2aa07 A high-severity flaw in Amazon Q Developer allowed a malicious code repository to silently execute commands on a developer’s machine and steal their AWS credentials. Wiz Research discovered the vulnerability, tracked as CVE-2026-12957, and reported it to Amazon on April 20. Amazon patched the issue on May 12, and the disclosure went public today. The […] This story continues at The Next Web]]> Xprize founder says global surveillance is a good thing because humans behave better when they are being watched https://thenextweb.com/news/diamandis-xprize-surveillance-privacy-transparency Fri, 26 Jun 2026 16:13:25 +0000 TheNextWeb=026bfc4c45db72b17444d2618255ca57 Xprize Foundation founder Peter Diamandis has joined a growing list of tech executives who believe global surveillance is a good idea, writing on X this week that “humans behave better when they’re being watched.” In a Substack essay titled “Visibility, Transparency and Trust,” he described what he called “radical transparency” as inevitable and positive, envisioning […] This story continues at The Next Web]]>
